pr.title: RHAIENG-5021: params-env-odh CI validation perpetually fails for N (latest) images
pr.body:
The params-env-odh CI validation perpetually fails because the N
(latest) image references in params-latest.env point to moving targets
that get rebuilt on each merge. Images may not exist yet, sizes drift
beyond thresholds, and commit IDs go stale between updates. This masks
real regressions and blocks unrelated PRs.

Apply the same proven pattern RHOAI already uses: replace N image URLs
with dummy placeholders and skip image validation for params-latest.env
regardless of variant. The N images are replaced at deploy time anyway.
The uniqueness check in check_variables_uniq already excludes dummy
values, so the params record count validation continues to work.

Closes [RHAIENG-5021](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHAIENG-5021)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Jiri Danek <jdanek@redhat.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Updated CI validation logic to apply uniformly across manifest variants instead of variant-specific handling.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
